title: ModelRuntime capability introspection IPC (Tauri kernel.model_runtime.capabilities)
goal: Wire the Tauri IPC channel `kernel.model_runtime.capabilities(model_id)` returning the ModelCapabilities for a registered model. Frontend uses this to decide which Inference Lab UI toggles are visible (hidden, not greyed) per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES.
expected_diff_shape: New Tauri command in app/src-tauri/src/commands/model_runtime.rs registering `kernel_model_runtime_capabilities` (snake-case Tauri convention) backed by ModelRegistry::lookup; main.rs invoke_handler registration; corresponding TypeScript binding contract in app/src/lib/ipc/model_runtime.ts.
implementation_notes: Per AC-MODEL-CAP-DECL and AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES the frontend reads capabilities via `kernel.model_runtime.capabilities(model_id)` and hides (not greys) unsupported toggles. Tauri command signature: `#[tauri::command] async fn kernel_model_runtime_capabilities(model_id: String, state: tauri::State<'_, ModelRuntimeState>) -> Result<ModelCapabilities, String>` — parse model_id string into ModelId (Uuid v7), call registry.lookup, return the declared_capabilities. Companion command `kernel_model_runtime_list_loaded` returns Vec<{ model_id, runtime_binding, artifact_path, sha256, perf_stats }> for the §10.13 ModelRuntime Control Panel (AC-MODEL-RUNTIME-CONTROL-PANEL); the panel itself ships in cluster X but this IPC primitive is foundational. TypeScript binding in app/src/lib/ipc/model_runtime.ts: export `async function capabilities(modelId: string): Promise<ModelCapabilities>` calling `invoke('kernel_model_runtime_capabilities', { modelId })`. Type ModelCapabilities mirrors the Rust struct exactly (serde Serialize uses camelCase via #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] so frontend gets `supportsLora`, `supportsKvPrefixCache`, etc.). Backend inspector plane (HBR-VIS-004) MUST NOT mutate via this command — it's read-only. Error path returns String error for Tauri serialization compat; structured ModelRuntimeError gets logged via FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-CAPS-LOOKUP. Tests: invoke from a Tauri test harness with a registered model; receive expected capabilities JSON.
risk_if_missed: Frontend cannot decide toggle visibility; lab UI either shows all toggles greyed (wrong per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES) or hardcodes capability matrix (drifts from backend).
validator_focus: IPC contract is read-only; JSON shape uses camelCase for frontend; missing model_id returns explicit error (not panic); TypeScript binding type matches Rust struct byte-for-byte.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: IPC is read-only (no mutation path from frontend through this command).
TypeScript type-check passes (camelCase fields stay aligned).
Error path tested for unknown model_id.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Tauri IPC `kernel_model_runtime_capabilities(model_id)` correctly dispatches through live ModelRuntime adapter when attached, falls back to declared metadata otherwise, with explicit fail paths for unknown model and non-v7 UUID. ModelCapabilitiesIpc DTO is camelCase per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES; core type stays snake_case (verified in MT-071 capabilities_core_snake_case_and_ipc_projection_camelcase_are_both_stable). list_loaded projection filters by registry.is_loaded. Locks are RwLock with poisoned-lock recovery to string errors (no panic across IPC boundary).
